_meta.description: Reusable appeal frameworks linked to denial codes. Each strategy is a clinical-reviewed, actionable blueprint.

records.name: Medical Necessity Internal Appeal
records.summary: Structured internal appeal citing treating physician support, clinical guidelines, and peer-reviewed literature to establish medical necessity for denied services.
use_cases: Inpatient admission denied as not medically necessary
Specialist visit denied without clinical justification
DME denied as not medically necessary
Behavioral health treatment denied as not medically necessary
contraindications: Do not use if denial is due to billing error — use corrected claim workflow instead
Do not assert specific outcome predictions in appeal letter
records.notes: Primary strategy for CO-50. insurance-appeal-generator tool implements this. Clinical review required before activation of new variations.

records.name: Coverage Dispute — Plan Document Review
records.summary: Disputes non-covered charges by citing specific plan document language, Summary of Benefits, and applicable federal coverage mandates.
use_cases: Service denied as not covered when plan language is ambiguous
ACA essential health benefit denied as non-covered
Mental health service denied under less favorable terms than medical services
contraindications: Do not claim legal violations — describe what plan documents say and let the regulator determine compliance
records.notes: MHPAEA (Mental Health Parity) is the strongest angle for behavioral health denials coded as non-covered.

records.name: Retroactive Prior Authorization Appeal
records.summary: Requests retroactive authorization approval by citing medical necessity, emergency circumstances, or provider good-faith reliance on authorization guidance.
use_cases: Emergency admission where prior auth was not obtainable
Provider requested auth but received incorrect guidance
Authorization was obtained but not applied to claim correctly
contraindications: Not applicable if service was genuinely elective and auth was knowingly skipped
records.notes: Emergency exception is strongest angle. ACA requires coverage of emergency services without prior auth.

records.name: Corrected Claim Submission
records.summary: Identifies and corrects specific billing or coding errors that caused the denial, then resubmits as a corrected claim to preserve the original filing date.
use_cases: Missing NPI, auth number, or modifier on original claim
Wrong ICD-10 code submitted — correct specificity needed
Outdated CPT code submitted for date of service
Missing required attachment on original submission
contraindications: Do not resubmit as new claim — resubmit as corrected claim to preserve original filing date
Do not use this strategy when denial is clinical (medical necessity) not administrative
records.notes: Lowest complexity strategy. High success rate for administrative denials. No AI generation needed in most cases.

records.name: Age/Sex Restriction Override — Peer-to-Peer Review
records.summary: Requests peer-to-peer clinical review between the treating physician and payer medical director to challenge algorithmic age or sex-based coverage restrictions using patient-specific clinical evidence.
use_cases: Screening denied based on age threshold (e.g., colonoscopy at age 40 vs standard 45)
Pediatric service denied as typically adult-only
ACA preventive service denied under age restriction
contraindications: Peer-to-peer must be requested by treating physician — patient cannot directly request this
records.notes: ACA preventive services mandate: USPSTF A/B rated preventive services must be covered without cost-sharing. Reference USPSTF guidelines directly.
